4. Let us now turn to Varahamihira who suggested the
pre-natal epoch theory in one verse of his Brihajjataka.

Pre-Natal Epoch 81
The birth of a child takes place in the sign occupied by the
Moon. This is represented by the number of the dvadasamsa
(1112 part) occupied by the Moon at that time. The day or
night and the ghatis of birth time must be known by the
number of ghatis at the time of the question and the nature of
the sign as diurnal or nocturnal.
The Moon occupies some sign at the time of conception or
of birth. Find the dvadasamsa of the Moon. Count from the
Moon's position this dvadasamsa sign. The birth takes place
when the Moon is in that sign. Suppose the conception took
place when Aries was rising and when the Moon was in the
tenth house Makara. lfthe Moon is in the tenth dvadasamsa,
that sign will be Tula.
